---
title: "05 · Loss, optimization, backpropagation"
description: "Planned · How models learn. Loss functions, gradient descent, Adam, backprop derived end-to-end on a 2-layer MLP."
author: "Prerna Tyagi"
date: "2026-05-28"
categories: [planned, training, backprop, optimization]
reading-time: true
toc: false
---

<p class="breadcrumb-mono">[← series](../../)</p>

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Planned.** Part of *Building Blocks of Modern AI*. This post is the literal mechanism behind the signature line — *loss decreases with optimized iterations* is the algorithm.
:::

## Planned coverage

- The loss function family — MSE, cross-entropy, contrastive — when each is right
- **Gradient descent** — vanilla, SGD, mini-batch
- **Backpropagation** — chain rule, derived end-to-end on a 2-layer MLP
- **Adam, AdamW, momentum** — what each fixes vs vanilla SGD
- Learning rate schedules — warmup, cosine; the production defaults
- Vanishing / exploding gradients — why they happen, what fixes them
- From-scratch implementation in NumPy, then PyTorch
